Description.SourceString:
So this is what the people who left Bramblemead are up to. Honestly, it seems like a pretty nice life. There’s amalgamated and, for lack of a better word, normal people working side by side. Shops are well-stocked, and fields are filled with delicious looking vegetables. The entire landscape is a dazzling display of vibrant colours and … oh, yeah, they have dragons.

Not dragons like we had – the ones that burn villages to the ground and stomp on people for fun. No, these dragons watch over the villagers, help them with construction, farming, all sorts of stuff. They even give them this weird little thing they call a Blessing, which somehow binds them together in a way I’m seriously going to need to study later.

The people don’t worship the dragons either. There’s nothing like the goblin shenanigans down south. No, the traditional religions line up in a multifaith community with one another. Saradominists greet Zamorakians and no one starts a fight. I assume, anyway, no one really wears little badges or anything, but there are shrines to more than just Saradomin here, so I like to daydream.

I set up a little shop. Can you imagine? Me, with a shop! Just selling little potions and salves, and services for those that need to speak with the dead. Simple stuff, nothing too taxing, but enough to put a roof over my head.

I think I could be happy here. Well, as happy as I get.
